title: How the plan briefing reads
question: How verbose should the /decision-tree plan briefing be?
rationale: Keep the four-section structure people already scan (Related, Constraints, Where it fits, Call), but cap every entry to one line and drop empty sections instead of padding them with 'none'. Fixes the wordiness without making readers relearn the shape. Lives in the shared skill template (consulting.md), so it applies to every project using the skill.

tradeoffs.note: same four-section shape readers already know; nothing to relearn
one line per entry, empty sections dropped, fits on a screen without scrolling
much shorter than before but not the leanest possible

options.label: Lead with the call, then terse bullets

tradeoffs.note: the recommendation lands first, fastest to act on
dropping sections by default can hide a constraint that mattered
no structure to jump around; everything must be read in order
